name: pv86
scope: IA-32 SDM Vol.3 method-1 / VME-OFF subset (VME/VIF/VIP + the interrupt-redirection bitmap are OUT of scope, documented follow-on; method-1 routes every IOPL-sensitive op through the CPL0 monitor and is architecturally correct).
transitions: real->protected (CR0.PE 0->1, CS far-jump to 32-bit flat code segment 0x08)
GDT: null + code0 0x08 + data0 0x10 + 2 data0 clones + TSS32 0x28 (base 0x3000, limit 0x67)
TR load: LTR 0x28 (TSS.SS0=0x10, ESP0=0x00088000 = the CPL0 monitor stack)
IDT load (base=0x1000 limit=0x7FF) + 1 32-bit INTERRUPT gate: vector 13 (#GP) -> gp_handler (the V86 monitor), target CS 0x08 DPL0
copy the V86 task blob (image-resident, LMA high) down to linear 0x00010000 (V86 segment 0x1000)
paging enable (CR3=0x11000, CR0.PG 0->1; 4 KiB pages; identity map [0,0x40000)+[0x80000,0x91000)+code window [0xF0000,0x100000), all P|RW|US so the CPL3 V86 task may walk them)
ENTER V86: IRET a 9-word V86 frame (GS=FS=0,DS=ES=SS=0x2000,ESP=0xF000,EFLAGS=VM|bit1,CS=0x1000,EIP=0) -> EFLAGS.VM 0->1, CS:EIP=0x1000:0000, CPL 0->3, paging still live
V86 segmentation: the V86 task writes 0xCAFEBABE to DS:0 with DS=0x2000 -> linear 0x20000 (DS<<4 base, NO GDT read)

IOPL-sensitive #GP x6: CLI/STI/PUSHF/POPF/INT 0x21/INT 0x20 at IOPL=0 (<3) each -> #GP(0) -> the 9-word V86 frame pushed on TSS.SS0:ESP0, EFLAGS.VM CLEARED on entry, DS/ES/FS/GS zeroed; gp_handler (CPL0) decodes the V86 opcode, logs it, advances EIP, and IRETs back into V86 (VM 0->1)
TERMINATE: the V86 task's INT 0x20 is recognised by the monitor as the done sentinel -> isa-debug-exit (out 0xf4, 0x42 -> qemu status 133)

delivery_records._note: Record numbers from the generated golden (gen_trace.py --system, single-stepped, 949 records). Validated by run-sys-golden.sh step 5e + the RTL --system diff in step 7 (EQUIVALENT 949/949). IMPLEMENT NOTE: the Implement phase fixed pv86.ld so .reset (the 16-bit reset stub) gets an explicit AT(IMAGE_BASE+0xFFF0) LMA — the original .ld left .reset with an out-of-image LMA so objcopy -O binary TRUNCATED the `ljmp $0xF000,$_start16_off` off (0xFFF0 zero-filled) and the boot relied on 8 zero-byte `add` records segment-offset-wrapping at 0x10000 -> _start16. With the reset stub in place the boot far-jumps directly to _start16 (pc->0), shaving the 8 wrap records (956 -> 949). The golden was regenerated deterministically; the V86 machinery exercised is identical.
delivery_records.v86_entry: n=692 cs 0x0008->0x1000 eflags 0x00000046->0x00020002 (EFLAGS.VM 0->1, bit17 set) eip->0x0 (the V86 task), ss 0x0010->0x2000, ds->0x2000; this is the IRET into V86 (CPL 0->3, paging still live CR0.PG=1). n=693 pc=0x0 begins the V86 task (VM=1).
delivery_records.v86_seg_store: n=696 eax=0xcafebabe (loaded), n=697 pc=0xd: the V86 task's `mov %eax,%ds:0` (66 a3 00 00 = the 16-bit-address moffs store form) committed with DS=0x2000 -> linear 0x20000 (DS<<4 base, NO GDT read) — proves V86 sel<<4 segmentation. Read back at n=946 esi=0xcafebabe.
delivery_records.gp_cli: n=698 CLI (0xFA) -> #GP -> gp_handler: cs 0x1000->0x08, ss 0x2000->0x10 (TSS.SS0:ESP0 stack switch), eflags 0x00020002->0x00000002 (EFLAGS.VM 1->0 CLEARED on entry), ds zeroed to 0x0000 (V86->PM delivery zeros DS/ES/FS/GS). The 9-word V86 frame (GS,FS,DS,ES,SS,ESP,EFLAGS,CS,EIP + #GP errcode 0) is on the monitor stack.
delivery_records.gp_cli_return: n=735 cs 0x08->0x1000 eflags 0x2->0x00020002 (VM 0->1) — the monitor IRETs the 9-word V86 frame back into V86, resuming the task at the next op.
delivery_records.gp_int21: n=865 INT 0x21 (0xCD 0x21) -> #GP; the monitor records vector 0x21 and advances EIP +2 (return n=902)
delivery_records.gp_int20_terminate: n=903 INT 0x20 (0xCD 0x20) -> #GP -> the monitor recognises the terminate sentinel and runs the success-proof accumulation + isa-debug-exit (no return).
delivery_records.success_proof: n=937 ecx=0x6 (total V86 #GP deliveries) eax=0x1 (CLI count); n=944 ebx=0x02010101 (STI=1 | PUSHF=1<<8 | POPF=1<<16 | INTn=2<<24); n=945 edx=0x20 (last INT vector); n=946 esi=0xcafebabe (the V86-entered sentinel read back from linear 0x20000); n=947 eax=0x42 (isa-debug-exit code); n=948 edx=0xf4 (out 0xf4 -> qemu status 133).

description: Bare-metal real->protected->PAGING->TSS->VIRTUAL-8086 demonstrator for the M7.2 gate. Boots from F000:FFF0 in 16-bit real mode, builds a 6-entry GDT (null + flat CPL0 code 0x08 + flat CPL0 data 0x10 + 2 data clones + a 32-bit available TSS at 0x28, base 0x00003000 / limit 0x67), sets CR0.PE and far-jumps into the 32-bit flat CPL0 code segment, loads the CPL0 data selectors + the boot stack, zeroes a marker area at 0x2000. It builds a 104-byte 32-bit TSS at linear 0x3000 with SS0=0x10 and ESP0=0x00088000 (a DEDICATED CPL0 monitor stack the #GP-from-V86 delivery switches to) and LTR 0x28 loads TR. It builds an IDT at linear 0x1000 with a single 32-bit INTERRUPT gate at vector 13 (#GP) -> gp_handler (the V86 monitor, target CS 0x08 DPL0). It copies the V86 task body (assembled at run-VMA 0x00010000 but stored high in the BIOS image at its LMA) down to linear 0x00010000 so the V86 task runs with CS=0x1000 / offset 0. It then builds a 2-level page hierarchy (4 KiB pages): PD[0]->PT0 identity-mapping [0,0x40000) (the V86 task 0x10000, the V86 data/stack windows 0x20000/0x2F000, the low structures, the page tables) + [0x80000,0x91000) (the monitor stacks) + the code window [0xF0000,0x100000), EVERY PTE/PDE carrying U/S=1 (0x7 = P|RW|US) because V86 runs at CPL3 (USER) and the pages must grant user access or the V86 fetch/data access #PFs. After CR3=0x11000 and CR0.PG=1 it ENTERS virtual-8086 mode: it builds a 9-word V86 IRET frame on the CPL0 stack (GS=0, FS=0, DS=0x2000, ES=0x2000, SS=0x2000, ESP=0xF000, EFLAGS=VM(bit17)|bit1, CS=0x1000, EIP=0) and IRETs -- because EFLAGS.VM is set in the popped image, the CPU enters V86 at CS:EIP=0x1000:0000 with CPL forced to 3, sel<<4 segment bases (CS base 0x10000, DS/SS base 0x20000), and paging still live (PE=1/PG=1). The V86 task (16-bit code at linear 0x10000) first writes 0xCAFEBABE to DS:0 with DS=0x2000 -> linear 0x20000, exercising V86 sel<<4 data segmentation with NO GDT descriptor read. It then executes the IOPL-sensitive sequence CLI / STI / PUSHF / POPF / INT 0x21 / INT 0x20: because IOPL=0 < 3, EACH of these raises #GP(0) and traps to the CPL0 monitor (the method-1 / VME-OFF behavior). On each delivery the CPU switches to TSS.SS0:ESP0, pushes the LARGER 9-word V86 exception frame (GS, FS, DS, ES, SS, ESP, EFLAGS, CS, EIP -- plus the #GP error code 0), CLEARS EFLAGS.VM (the monitor runs in flat 32-bit protected mode at CPL0), and ZEROES DS/ES/FS/GS. The gp_handler monitor reloads a flat DS, reads the faulting V86 opcode at linear (CS<<4)+EIP, logs it into a ring + per-opcode counters, advances the saved V86 EIP past the instruction (1 byte for CLI/STI/PUSHF/POPF, 2 bytes for INT n), discards the #GP error code, and IRETs the 9-word V86 frame back into V86 (EFLAGS.VM 0->1 again), resuming the V86 task. INT 0x20 is the terminate sentinel: the monitor accumulates the success proof into the GPRs (ecx=6 total deliveries, eax=1 CLI count, ebx packed STI|PUSHF<<8|POPF<<16|INTn<<24, edx=0x20 last INT vector, esi=0xCAFEBABE the V86-entered sentinel read back from linear 0x20000) and exits via out 0xf4 (isa-debug-exit -> qemu status 133). Exercises the M7.2 V86 machinery: V86 entry (EFLAGS.VM 0->1 + CPL 0->3 + sel<<4 bases) by IRET, V86 segmentation (sel<<4 base, no descriptor), the IOPL guard (IOPL<3 -> the IOPL-sensitive ops #GP to the monitor = method-1), the V86 interrupt/fault DELIVERY (the 9-word frame on the TSS.SS0:ESP0 stack, VM cleared, DS/ES/FS/GS zeroed, reusing the CPL3->CPL0 stack switch), and the IRET back into V86. VME/VIF/VIP + the interrupt-redirection bitmap are OUT of scope (documented follow-on); method-1 is architecturally correct. The RTL --system differential lands in Implement (the V86 RTL delta is de-risk-specified, gated behind a new `v86` so it is INERT when EFLAGS.VM=0).
